title: Oil Company Breaks Rules Many Times
summary: A company called Essar, which runs an oil refinery in Cheshire, England, has admitted breaking environmental rules almost 500 times. This happened between 2018 and 2020. Some of the times, they put too much cyanide, a harmful chemical, into a canal, river, and brook near their refinery. Cyanide can hurt animals and plants. The company said sorry and is working to fix the problems. They went to court because of these rules, and the Environment Agency, which protects nature, brought charges against them. The company claims there was no harm to wildlife, but breaking rules is serious to keep our environment safe.

background_read: Environmental rules are laws made to protect our planet, like keeping air and water clean. Companies must follow these rules to prevent pollution. Cyanide is a toxic substance that can harm living things if it gets into water. The Environment Agency in England watches over companies to make sure they don't break these rules. When rules are broken, companies might go to court to face consequences.
Article_Structure: WHO: Essar, a company that runs an oil refinery in Cheshire, England.
WHAT: Admitted breaking environmental rules nearly 500 times, including putting too much cyanide into water.
WHERE: At the Stanlow oil refinery in Ellesmere Port, Cheshire, affecting the Manchester Ship Canal, a river, and a brook.
WHY: To show that breaking rules can harm the environment, and companies need to be responsible.

perspectives.description: Breaking rules is bad because it risks polluting nature and hurting animals.
Essar apologized and is trying to fix the problems, saying they take it seriously.
